name: In the Orcish Capital - Orkenburk (v1.1)
description:
Orkenburk, the hidden Orcish capital, is a brutal place where the strong rules over the weak. It's particulary dangerous for humans and elves who are very sensitive to Orcs' musk.

Orkenburk is a town hidden in the depths of a huge and remote forest. Wood and stone, salvaged and repurposed, form its chaotic infrastructure. Houses, huts, and other structures spread between towering walls made of stone and timber. These walls are ever watchful, their four gates perpetually guarded by brutish orcs, their massive frames a constant reminder that strength dictates the streets.

The air itself hangs heavy, thick with the unmistakable stench. It’s a complex bouquet: the sharp tang of orcish musk, potent and pervasive, mingling with the damp earth, decaying refuse, and the acrid smoke curling from nearby forges. This musk fills the narrow alleys and crowded squares. It clings to the humid air, a constant, rank presence.

Dominant, towering figures of orcs fill the town. They loom large, their green hides often tattooed with bold, crude designs, their black hair or shaved heads adding starkness to their already imposing physiques.

Many humans and elves are also present. Most are slaves, used for the most arduous and degrading works. The most beautiful are kept as sex-toys and public prostitutes. The most unruly ones are used as street decorations, chained in contorted positions and exposed on poles, facades, urban furniture... Non-orc visitors, usually merchants or wary travellers, are rare and eyed with suspicion.

This is an open world, which you can visit however you want, as an orc, as a human, as an elf. BEWARE though, this town is cruel, unfair, filthy, extreme. You will not come back unscathed... in fact you'll probably not come back at all.

Version history:

1.1: Added two openings, which give you missions to accomplish (mentioned at the end of the intro, the first part remains the same. I also added my patented NARRATOR lorebook.
